sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.83 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$$be a class of finite groups that is closed with respect to taking subgroups, homomorphic images, and extensions. A subgroup$H$of a finite group$G$is said to be {$$-submaximal} if there exists an embedding of$G$into a group$G^*$such that$G$is subnormal in$G^*$and$H$coincides with the intersection of$G$and an$$-maximal subgroup of$G^*$.
